content.text:
# Orchestration TUI — Design Spec

## Overview

Two-layer orchestration visualization for pi's orchestrator extension.

- **Layer 1: Orchestration Plan** — replaces the TUI header. Pinned at top. Shows pipeline overview. Visible from task start → next task.
- **Layer 2: Subagent Tool Blocks** — per-subagent activity feed in chat history. Chronological order below the plan panel.

---

## Layer 1: Orchestration Plan (Header)

### Location
The Plan panel **replaces pi's built-in header** via `pi.setHeader(factory)`.
It renders at the very top of the terminal, above the chat area.
It does NOT scroll — it stays pinned as user scrolls through tool blocks.

### Lifecycle

| Event | Action |
|-------|--------|
| `delegate`/`parallel`/`chain` tool called | Build plan panel with goal + steps. Call `setHeader(planComponent)`. Start elapsed timer. |
| Subagent completes | Update step to ✓ with duration. Update progress dots. |
| All subagents done | All steps show ✓. Elapsed timer stops. Panel stays visible. |
| User types next task → `before_agent_start` fires | Call `setHeader(undefined)` to restore built-in header. Ready for next plan. |

### Visual

```
┌─ Orchestration Plan ──────────────────────────────┐
│ Goal: add browsing to pi with cloakbrowser          │
│  ✓ Check cloakbrowser API                           │
│  ⠋ Implement browse integration                     │
│  ○ Review implementation                            │
│  ●●● [1/3]      •    Elapsed: 45s                   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Elements

| Element | Content | Source |
|---------|---------|--------|
| Header | `┌─ Orchestration Plan ──┐` | Static decoration |
| Goal | `Goal: <task description>` | From `delegate()`/`parallel()`/`chain()` call params |
| Pipeline steps | `✓ Check cloakbrowser API` | Task-level steps from orchestrator. NOT specialist names. |
| Progress dots | `●●● [1/3]` | Filled = completed. Current = filled with spinner step. Empty = pending. |
| Elapsed | `Elapsed: 45s` | Live counter. Updates via `setInterval(1s)`. |

### Plan steps vs specialist names

The Plan panel shows **task names**, not specialist names:

| Plan panel | Tool block header |
|------------|-------------------|
| `✓ Check cloakbrowser API` | `╭─ Researcher ──╮` |
| `✓ Implement browse integration` | `╭─ Coder ──╮` |
| `○ Review implementation` | `╭─ Reviewer ──╮` |

### Status Transitions

```
Initial:        ○ Check API  ○ Implement  ○ Review       ○○○ [0/3]  Elapsed: 0s
Scout starts:   ○ Check API  ○ Implement  ○ Review       ○○○ [0/3]  Elapsed: 2s
First tool:     ⠋ Check API  ○ Implement  ○ Review       ●○○ [0/3]  Elapsed: 5s
Scout done:     ✓ Check API  ○ Implement  ○ Review       ●○○ [1/3]  Elapsed: 14s
Coder starts:   ✓ Check API  ⠋ Implement  ○ Review       ●●○ [1/3]  Elapsed: 16s
Coder done:     ✓ Check API  ✓ Implement  ○ Review       ●●○ [2/3]  Elapsed: 45s
Reviewer done:  ✓ Check API  ✓ Implement  ✓ Review       ●●● [3/3]  Elapsed: 58s
```

---

## Layer 2: Subagent Tool Blocks (Chat History)

### Location
Standard `ToolExecutionComponent` with `renderShell: "self"`.
Renders in the chat area below the plan panel. Scrollable. Permanent in history.

### Visual — During execution

```
╭─ Researcher ──────────────────────────────────────╮
│ ┌─ Task ───────────────┐                           │
│ │ check cloakbrowser    │                           │
│ └──────────────────────┘                           │
│ ●●● [3/3]                                          │
│ ✓ scan pi web tools (8s)                            │
│ ✓ check cloakbrowser api (12s)                      │
│ ⠋ synthesize findings...                            │
╰────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### Visual — After completion

```
╭─ Researcher ──────────────────────────────────────╮
│ ┌─ Task ───────────────┐                           │
│ │ check cloakbrowser    │                           │
│ └──────────────────────┘                           │
│ ●●● [3/3]                                          │
│ ✓ scan pi web tools (8s)                            │
│ ✓ check cloakbrowser api (12s)                      │
│ ✓ synthesize findings (5s)                          │
╰────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

**Key rule: The activity feed NEVER collapses.** Both during and after execution, the same layout is shown. The only difference:
- During: current step shows `⠋` spinner instead of `✓`
- After: all steps show `✓` with durations

### Elements

| Element | Content | Source |
|---------|---------|--------|
| Header | `╭─ Researcher ──╮` | Specialist name. **NOT** "delegate" or the tool name. |
| Task box | `┌─ Task ──┐ / │ check... │ / └──┘` | From subagent activity feed |
| Progress dots | `●●● [3/3]` | From activity feed |
| Steps | `✓ scan pi web tools (8s)` | From activity feed. Shows ✓ when done, ⠋ when current, ○ when pending. |
| Spinner | `⠋` on current step during execution | From activity feed |

---

## Full Layout (terminal view)

```
┌─ Orchestration Plan ──────────────────────────────┐  ← PINNED (setHeader)
│ Goal: add browsing to pi with cloakbrowser          │    NEVER scrolls
│  ✓ Check cloakbrowser API                           │    Visible until next task
│  ✓ Implement browse integration                     │
│  ○ Review implementation                            │
│  ●●○ [2/3]      •    Elapsed: 45s                   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                     │  ← Chat area
│ ╭─ Researcher ──────────────────────────────────╮   │    Scrolls below plan
│ │ ┌─ Task ───────────────┐                       │   │
│ │ │ check cloakbrowser    │                       │   │
│ │ └──────────────────────┘                       │   │
│ │ ●●● [3/3]                                      │   │
│ │ ✓ scan pi web tools (8s)                        │   │
│ │ ✓ check cloakbrowser api (12s)                  │   │
│ │ ✓ synthesize findings (5s)                      │   │
│ ╰────────────────────────────────────────────────╯   │
│                                                     │
│ ╭─ Coder ────────────────────────────────────────╮  │
│ │ ┌─ Task ───────────────┐                       │   │
│ │ │ implement browse      │                       │   │
│ │ └──────────────────────┘                       │   │
│ │ ●○ [1/2]                                       │   │
│ │ ✓ read cloakbrowser files (3s)                  │   │
│ │ ⠋ write integration code...                     │   │
│ ╰────────────────────────────────────────────────╯   │
│                                                     │
│ ✓ Orchesrator synthesized: The best way to add...   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ > _                                                 │  ← Editor bar
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Changes from current code

### `renderCall` — Tool block header

**Current:**
```
→ scout
```

**Fixed:**
```
╭─ Researcher ──────────────────────────────────────╮
```

### `renderResult` — Final state

**Current (isPartial=false):**
```
✓ scout (33 turns)
```

**Fixed (isPartial=false):**
Same as activity feed, all steps ✓ with durations. No collapse.

### New: Plan panel component

**Not implemented yet.** Build a Component that:
1. Renders as a string array forming the plan box
2. Updates elapsed time via `setInterval(1s)`
3. Replaces the header via `pi.setHeader(planComponent)`
4. Restores built-in header via `pi.setHeader(undefined)` on `before_agent_start`

---

## Data Flow

```
User task
  │
  ▼
orchestrator.ts: execute()
  │  resetOrchestratorActivity()
  │  build plan panel from goal + steps
  │  pi.setHeader(() => planComponent)              ← Header replaced
  │  onUpdate({ content: [activity feed] })          ← Tool blocks start
  │
  ▼
orchestrator.ts: runSubagent()
  │  session.subscribe() handles events:
  │    message_update → parseTextForFeed()
  │    tool_call      → addSubstep()
  │    tool_result    → completeLastSubstep()
  │    message_end    → completeCurrentStep()
  │
  │  Each handler:
  │    → onUpdate({ content: [feed] })               ← Tool block updates
  │    → update plan panel step state                 ← Plan header updates
  │
  ▼
orchestrator.ts: execute() returns
  │  renderResult(isPartial=false) returns activity feed  ← Tool block final
  │  plan panel shows all ✓                               ← Header final
  │
  ▼
User types next task
  │
  ▼
orchestrator.ts: before_agent_start
  │  pi.setHeader(undefined)                           ← Built-in header restored
  │  (ready for next plan)
```

---

## State Management

```typescript
// Module-level state, reset per orchestration
let orchestrationGoal: string = "";
let orchestrationSteps: Array<{
  label: string;           // Task-level step name (e.g. "Check cloakbrowser API")
  specialist: string;      // Who's working on it
  completed: boolean;
  startTime: number;
  endTime?: number;
}> = [];
let orchestrationStartTime: number = 0;
let planTimer: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null = null;
let planComponent: Component | null = null;
```

---

## Rendering Functions

### `renderPlanPanel(goal, steps, elapsed) → Component`

Creates a Container with:
```
┌─ Orchestration Plan ──────────────────────────────┐
│ Goal: add browsing to pi with cloakbrowser          │
│  ✓ Check cloakbrowser API                           │
│  ⠋ Implement browse integration                     │
│  ○ Review implementation                            │
│  ●●● [1/3]      •    Elapsed: 45s                   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### `renderToolBlockHeader(specialistName) → string`
```
╭─ Researcher ──────────────────────────────────────╮
```

### `renderActivityFeed(specialistName, feedState) → string` (already exists)
Same as current `renderCombinedProgress`/`renderActivityFeed`. No changes needed.

---

## Edge Cases

| Case | Behavior |
|------|----------|
| Single `delegate` | Plan panel shows 1 step. Resolves when done. |
| `parallel` mode | Plan panel shows all tasks as separate steps. All update independently. |
| `chain` mode | Plan panel shows steps in order. Updates after each step. |
| User interrupt (Ctrl+C) | Plan panel freezes. Next task restores header. |
| Subagent error | Step shows `✗` prefix. Plan panel continues to next step. |
| Very long task names | Truncate to fit panel width with `...`. |
| New task mid-orchestration | `before_agent_start` fires → `setHeader(undefined)` → old plan gone. New plan starts fresh. |
| No orchestration running | Built-in header shows normally. |

---

## Files Changed

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `~/.pi/agent/extensions/orchestrator.ts` | Add `renderPlanPanel()` as a Component. Add `setHeader`/`setHeader(undefined)` calls. Add elapsed timer. Modify `renderCall` for specialist name header frame. Modify `renderResult` final to show activity feed instead of collapsing. |
| No core pi changes needed | `setHeader(factory)`, `renderShell: "self"`, `onUpdate` all exist in extension API. |

 ### The Problem                                                                                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                                                                                      
 fetch_content (pi's built-in URL fetcher) uses HTTP — it cannot render JS, bypass Cloudflare, or handle bot detection. CloakBrowser solves this: it's a real Chromium that passes

 ### Integration Paths (ranked by fit)                                                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                                                                                      
 #### 1. Custom browser Tool — Best Fit                                                                                                                                               
                                                                                                                                                                                      
 Register a dedicated tool the LLM calls when fetch_content fails. Uses Cloak's cloakserve (CDP multiplexer) over HTTP/WebSocket:                                                     
                                                                                                                                                                                      
 ```typescript                                                                                                                                                                        
   // In a pi extension                                                                                                                                                               
   pi.registerTool({                                                                                                                                                                  
     name: "browser_fetch",                                                                                                                                                           
     label: "Browser Fetch",                                                                                                                                                          
     description: "Fetch a URL using CloakBrowser stealth Chromium. Use when curl/fetch_content gets blocked by Cloudflare or JS-rendered pages.",                                    
     parameters: Type.Object({                                                                                                                                                        
       url: Type.String({ description: "The URL to fetch" }),                                                                                                                         
       waitUntil: Type.Optional(StringEnum(["load", "networkidle"])),                                                                                                                 
       screenshot: Type.Optional(Type.Boolean()),                                                                                                                                     
     }),                                                                                                                                                                              
     async execute(toolCallId, params, signal, onUpdate, ctx) {                                                                                                                       
       // Call cloakserve HTTP API (runs as sidecar process)                                                                                                                          
       // cloakserve listens on localhost:9222                                                                                                                                        
       // Connect via Playwright connect_over_cdp                                                                                                                                     
       const result = await fetch("http://localhost:9222/json/version");                                                                                                              
       // ... launch page, navigate, extract content, screenshot                                                                                                                      
       onUpdate({ type: "status", message: "Navigating..." });                                                                                                                        
       // Return text content + optional screenshot                                                                                                                                   
       return { content: [{ type: "text", text: pageContent }] };                                                                                                                     
     },                                                                                                                                                                               
   });                                                                                                                                                                                
 ```                                                                                                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                                                                                      
 Why this wins: Clear LLM awareness ("I need a real browser"), typed params, no conflict with normal bash operations.

Override fetch_content via Event Interception                                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                                                                                      
 Intercept the fetch_content tool call and route through CloakBrowser when HTTP fetch fails:

 ### The Clean Integration                                                                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                                                                                      
 ```typescript                                                                                                                                                                        
   // pi extension — imports cloakbrowser directly, zero server                                                                                                                       
   import { launch } from "cloakbrowser";                                                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                                                      
   pi.registerTool({                                                                                                                                                                  
     name: "browser_fetch",                                                                                                                                                           
     label: "Browser Fetch",

       screenshot: Type.Optional(Type.Boolean()),                                                                                                                                     
     }),                                                                                                                                                                              
     async execute(toolCallId, params, signal, onUpdate, ctx) {                                                                                                                       
       const browser = await launch({ headless: true });                                                                                                                              
       const page = await browser.newPage();                                                                                                                                          
       await page.goto(params.url, { waitUntil: "networkidle" });                                                                                                                     
       const text = await page.evaluate(() => document.body.innerText);                                                                                                               
       const screenshot = params.screenshot                                                                                                                                           
         ? await page.screenshot({ type: "png", fullPage: true })                                                                                                                     
         : null;                                                                                                                                                                      
       await browser.close();                                                                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                      
       return {                                                                                                                                                                       
         content: [                                                                                                                                                                   
           { type: "text", text },                                                                                                                                                    
           ...(screenshot ? [{ type: "image", data: screenshot.toString("base64") }] : []),                                                                                           
         ],                                                                                                                                                                           
       };                                                                                                                                                                             
     },
 ```                                                                                                                                                                                  

 - Humanize — launch({ humanize: true }) for realistic typing/scrolling                                                                                                               
 - Proxy — launch({ proxy: "http://user:pass@proxy:8080" })                                                                                                                           
 - Per-call isolation — fresh browser instance per tool call, no state leaks

> pi can create extensions. Ask it to build one for your use case.

# Extensions

Extensions are TypeScript modules that extend pi's behavior. They can subscribe to lifecycle events, register custom tools callable by the LLM, add commands, and more.

> **Placement for /reload:** Put extensions in `~/.pi/agent/extensions/` (global) or `.pi/extensions/` (project-local) for auto-discovery. Use `pi -e ./path.ts` only for quick tests. Extensions in auto-discovered locations can be hot-reloaded with `/reload`.

**Key capabilities:**
- **Custom tools** - Register tools the LLM can call via `pi.registerTool()`
- **Event interception** - Block or modify tool calls, inject context, customize compaction
- **User interaction** - Prompt users via `ctx.ui` (select, confirm, input, notify)
- **Custom UI components** - Full TUI components with keyboard input via `ctx.ui.custom()` for complex interactions
- **Custom commands** - Register commands like `/mycommand` via `pi.registerCommand()`
- **Session persistence** - Store state that survives restarts via `pi.appendEntry()`
- **Custom rendering** - Control how tool calls/results and messages appear in TUI

**Example use cases:**
- Permission gates (confirm before `rm -rf`, `sudo`, etc.)
- Git checkpointing (stash at each turn, restore on branch)
- Path protection (block writes to `.env`, `node_modules/`)
- Custom compaction (summarize conversation your way)
- Conversation summaries (see `summarize.ts` example)
- Interactive tools (questions, wizards, custom dialogs)
- Stateful tools (todo lists, connection pools)
- External integrations (file watchers, webhooks, CI triggers)
- Games while you wait (see `snake.ts` example)

See [examples/extensions/](../examples/extensions/) for working implementations.

Here's a summary of all fixes applied to `/Users/shivam94/.pi/agent/extensions/orchestrator.ts`:

## Fix 1: Plan Panel Header — Container lifecycle

**`buildPlanPanel`** reused `new Container()` every factory call, orphan-ing the timer-updated instance:

```typescript
// Before: new container every time
function buildPlanPanel(tui, theme) {
    planContainer = new Container();  // ← orphaned old one
    ...
}

// After: reuse existing
function buildPlanPanel(tui, theme) {
    if (!planContainer) planContainer = new Container();  // ← stable ref
    ...
}
```

## Fix 2: Plan Panel Header — `mode === "tui"` guard

`setupPlanPanel` and `clearPlanPanel` now only call `ctx.ui.setHeader()` in TUI mode, matching the [custom-header example](https://github.com/...):

```typescript
function clearPlanPanel(ctx) {
    ...
    if (ctx.mode === "tui") ctx.ui.setHeader(undefined);
}

function setupPlanPanel(goal, stepLabels, ctx) {
    ...
    if (ctx.mode === "tui") ctx.ui.setHeader(factory);
}
```

## Fix 3: Tool block rendering — bordered box

**`renderResult`** now wraps the activity feed text in `│` borders + `╰─` bottom, matching the design spec:

```
╭─ Researcher ──────────────────────────────╮   ← renderCall
│  ⠋ Starting...                             │   ← renderResult wrapped
╰────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

`wrapInBox()` helper:
- Strips ANSI codes from content lines
- Pads each line to `boxWidth - 4` characters
- Adds `│ ... │` borders and `╰─...╯` bottom

## Fix 4: `theme.fg()` out of scope in `execute()`

The parallel mode's `updateStatus()` was calling `theme.fg()` inside `execute()` — `theme` is only available in `renderCall`/`renderResult`. **This would throw ReferenceError at runtime.** Removed the ANSI coloring from the status update (cosmetic only).

## Fix 5: Avoid `this` binding issues in helpers

Extracted `wrapInBox`, `boxWidthFor`, `specDisplayName` as **standalone closure functions** inside the extension factory function, not as object methods. The pi framework calls `renderResult` without `this` context.

## Fix 6: `parallel` and `chain` tools also got `renderShell: "self"` + boxed rendering

These dedicated tools had no custom rendering at all — now they also render with bordered boxes via `wrapInBoxStatic()`.

---

**Result:** The Plan panel header now shows at the top (in TUI mode), and tool blocks render as proper bordered boxes matching the design spec.
